\boxed{D}

The correct symbolization translates the sentence "If either George enrolls or Harry enrolls, then Ira does not enroll" into logical terms as **(G ∨ H) ⊃ ~I**, where the antecedent is the disjunction of G and H, and the consequent is the negation of I. This matches option D. The other options either misplace the negation or incorrectly structure the implication.
